blog.title: YouFly Blog
blog.subtitle: Travel tips, guides and useful information for your flights.
blog.ctaSubtitle: Search cheap flights from Chisinau with real-time prices.
blog.seoDesc: Practical travel tips, destination guides and strategies to fly cheaper from Moldova.
blog.art1Title: How to find cheap airline tickets in 2026
blog.art1Excerpt: Discover proven strategies to reduce the cost of flights by up to 50%. From booking timing to alternative airports.
blog.art2Title: Top 10 affordable destinations from Moldova in 2026
blog.art2Excerpt: From Turkish beaches to European capitals — the most popular destinations chosen by Moldovans and average ticket prices.
blog.art3Excerpt: Rules for each airline, tricks to fit everything in 10 kg and the updated list of prohibited items.
blog.art4Title: Direct flight vs. with layover: which is worth more?
blog.art4Excerpt: We analyze the costs, comfort and risks of each option. When is it worth choosing connecting flights and how to manage them.
blog.art5Excerpt: Real situations where insurance can save you from huge expenses. What it covers, what it doesn't and how to choose the best option.
blog.art6Title: Istanbul in 3 days: the complete traveler's guide
blog.art6Excerpt: The optimal route, local restaurants, must-see attractions and practical tips for the most vibrant metropolis on the Bosphorus.
blog.art1Body: Finding cheap flight tickets requires a combination of timing, flexibility, and the right tools. The best strategy is to book 6-8 weeks in advance for European destinations and 3-4 months for long-haul flights. Always clear your browser cookies or use incognito mode when searching repeatedly — some booking sites track your visits and may increase prices. Set price alerts for your desired routes so you are notified when fares drop. Flying on Tuesdays and Wednesdays is typically 20-30% cheaper than weekend flights. Consider nearby airports — flying into a secondary airport and taking a train can save significant money. Budget airlines like Wizz Air and Ryanair offer incredible deals if you book early and pack light. Connecting flights are often 40-60% cheaper than direct routes. Apps like Google Flights offer calendar views to spot the cheapest travel dates at a glance.
blog.art2Body: Moldova is a small but surprisingly vibrant country with a wealth of travel possibilities. Istanbul remains the top destination from Chisinau, offering a stunning blend of European and Asian culture, world-class cuisine, and iconic landmarks like the Hagia Sophia and Grand Bazaar. Barcelona draws travelers seeking beach and city culture, with direct Wizz Air connections making it very accessible. Vienna is perfect for a weekend cultural escape — the opera, museums, and coffee houses are second to none. London continues to be a top destination for the diaspora and tourists alike. Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam complete the top European destinations. For budget travelers, Bucharest and Kyiv offer vibrant city breaks within a few hours by flight. Seasonal deals to the Mediterranean — Malta, Greece, and Cyprus — are available spring through autumn.
blog.art3Body: Understanding carry-on baggage rules can save you significant money and stress at the airport. Each airline has different size and weight limits. Wizz Air allows one small bag (40x30x20 cm, max 10 kg) for free, but charges for a cabin bag. Ryanair permits one small bag (40x20x25 cm) under the seat at no cost, but charges for bags in the overhead bin. Turkish Airlines and Austrian Airlines include a cabin bag for free. Key tips: weigh your bag at home before leaving; use compression packing cubes to maximize space; wear your heaviest items on board; pack toiletries in 100ml containers in a clear bag; remove prohibited items such as liquids over 100ml, sharp objects, and large batteries. Arrive early to avoid being forced to check your bag at the gate, where fees are highest.
blog.art4Body: The choice between a connecting flight and a direct flight is one of the most common dilemmas travelers face. Direct flights cost more but save time and reduce stress — there is no risk of missing a connection, no waiting in transit, and your luggage travels with you seamlessly. Connecting flights can be 40-70% cheaper and are sometimes the only option for certain routes. When booking a connecting flight, allow at least 2 hours of transit time at European hubs and 3 hours at Middle Eastern airports. Check whether both legs are on the same booking — separate tickets mean the airline is not responsible if you miss your connection. Self-connecting through budget airline hubs can yield dramatic savings, but requires careful planning and adequate transit visas if applicable.
blog.art5Body: Travel insurance is one of the most overlooked yet important aspects of planning a trip. For a modest premium — often 10-30 EUR for a week in Europe — you get protection against medical emergencies, trip cancellation, lost luggage, and flight delays. The most critical coverage is medical, especially when traveling outside the EU where your EHIC card may not be accepted. Look for policies that include emergency medical evacuation, as hospital bills in the US, UAE, or Southeast Asia can reach tens of thousands of euros. Trip cancellation insurance is valuable if you have non-refundable bookings and something unexpected happens. Always read the exclusions carefully, particularly for extreme sports and pre-existing conditions, and check the excess before purchasing.
blog.art6Body: Istanbul is one of the world's most captivating cities and easy to explore in 3 days from Chisinau on a direct Wizz Air flight. Day 1: Start in Sultanahmet — visit the Hagia Sophia (free entry), the Blue Mosque, and Topkapi Palace. In the evening, take a sunset Bosphorus cruise and dine on grilled fish at the Galata Bridge. Day 2: Explore Beyoglu — walk the bustling Istiklal Avenue, visit the Galata Tower for panoramic views, and shop at the Grand Bazaar with over 4,000 shops. Day 3: Discover Uskudar on the Asian side for authentic Istanbul away from tourist crowds, then visit the Spice Bazaar. Budget tip: the Istanbul Museum Pass covers all major attractions. A 3-day trip including flights and accommodation can be done for under 200 EUR per person if booked in advance.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about YouFly bookings.
faq.stillHelpText: Did not find the answer you were looking for? Our team is available to help.
faq.a1: YouFly is a flight booking platform that lets you search and compare flights from hundreds of airlines in real time, at the best prices.
faq.a2: Yes! Displayed prices include all taxes and fees. There are no hidden charges. The price you see is the final price you will pay.
faq.q3: How long is an offer valid?
faq.a3: Offers are usually valid for 15 to 60 minutes. An expiry timer is shown on the passenger details page.
faq.q4: How can I find my booking?
faq.a4: You can access your booking at any time from the 'My Booking' page by entering the booking code received at confirmation. The code is 6 uppercase letters.
faq.q5: Is it safe to pay on YouFly?
faq.a5: YouFly is a demo platform — payment is simulated and no real transactions are processed. Card data is not stored or transmitted anywhere.
faq.q6: Can I book for multiple passengers?
faq.a6: Yes! You can book for adults, children (2-11 years) and infants (under 2 years). Select the number of passengers in the search form.
faq.a7: YouFly offers all 4 standard classes: Economy, Premium Economy, Business and First Class. Availability depends on the chosen flight.
faq.a8: You can reach us at +373 22 000 000 or by email at support{'@'}youfly.md. We are available 24/7.
faq.q9: What happens if my flight is cancelled?
faq.a9: In case of cancellation, you will be notified by the airline at the email address provided. Refund policy depends on the fare chosen.
faq.q10: Can I change or cancel my booking?
faq.a10: Changes and cancellations depend on the fare chosen. Some tickets are non-refundable, others allow changes for a fee. Contact us for assistance.

luggage.title: Baggage Rules
luggage.subtitle: Everything you need to know about baggage before your flight.
luggage.tip1Desc: Even if the bag looks small, exact dimensions matter. Low-cost carriers are strict.
luggage.tip2Desc: Overweight bags at the airport can cost 50 EUR+. Weigh them before departure.
luggage.tip3Desc: Put your contact details inside and outside. Avoid permanently losing your baggage.
luggage.seoDesc: Baggage rules for major airlines. Dimensions, weights, fees and prohibited items on board.
luggage.prohib_LiquidsRule: Max 100 ml per container, all in a transparent 1L bag (carry-on)
luggage.prohib_BatteriesRule: Max 100Wh in carry-on; >100Wh requires approval; prohibited in checked bags
luggage.prohib_SharpRule: Prohibited in carry-on; allowed in checked bags (protected)
luggage.prohib_SpraysRule: Security sprays, tear gas — completely prohibited
luggage.prohib_ExplosivesRule: Fireworks, ammunition, flammable substances — completely prohibited
luggage.prohib_MagnetsRule: Strong magnets that can disrupt navigation — prohibited

checkin.title: Online Check-in
checkin.subtitle: Complete guide to online check-in before your flight.
checkin.seoDesc: Complete guide to online check-in. Check-in windows, fees and tips for Wizz Air, Ryanair, Turkish Airlines and others.
checkin.step1Desc: You need: your booking reference (PNR), passport/ID card, and the email address you booked with.
checkin.step2Desc: Access the official airline website or mobile app. Avoid third-party sites!
checkin.step3Desc: Choose your preferred seat on the plane. Free seats are limited, premium seats cost extra.
checkin.step4Desc: Save the boarding pass to your phone (Apple Wallet / Google Pay) or print it. You'll need it at the airport!
checkin.tip1: Check in as soon as the window opens to choose the best seats for free.
checkin.tip2: Check if your fare allows online check-in — some very basic tickets don't.
checkin.tip3: At Ryanair, the penalty for airport check-in can be €55 per passenger!
checkin.tip4: A phone boarding pass is accepted at all major European airports.
checkin.tip5: Watch your baggage weight — check before leaving home.
checkin.checkInBtnLabel: Check in online with {airline} (opens in new tab)

visa.title: Visa Requirements
visa.subtitle: Information about visas required for Moldovan citizens.
visa.infoNoteSub: Visa rules change frequently. Always verify at the embassy or consulate of your destination before travelling.
visa.schengenText: Moldovan citizens with biometric passports can travel in the Schengen area without a visa for a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa.schengenNote: Important: the number of days is calculated cumulatively, not per country!
visa.seoDesc: Check if you need a visa for your destination. Updated guide for citizens of the Republic of Moldova.

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.seoDesc: Read the terms and conditions of use for the YouFly platform.
terms.s1: By accessing and using the YouFly platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions of Service. If you do not agree with any part of the terms, you cannot access the service.
terms.s2: YouFly is a flight search and booking platform that acts as an intermediary between users and air travel service providers via the Duffel API. All bookings are processed directly with the airlines.
terms.s3: All prices are displayed in real time and include applicable airport taxes and fees. YouFly does not charge additional commissions beyond the displayed price. Payment is processed securely. YouFly is a demo platform and does not process real payments.
terms.s4: The modification and cancellation policy for tickets depends on the fare chosen by the passenger. YouFly forwards modification/cancellation requests to the relevant airline. Refunds are processed according to the airline's policy.
terms.s5: YouFly is not responsible for: flight delays or cancellations, lost baggage, changes to flight schedules or any other events outside our control. Airlines are solely responsible for transport services.
terms.s6: Personal data collected during the booking process is used solely for processing bookings and complies with GDPR provisions. We do not sell or share your personal data with third parties, except for the airlines needed to process bookings.
terms.s7: For any questions about these terms, please contact us at legal{'@'}youfly.md or at +373 22 000 000.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.seoDesc: Find out how YouFly collects, uses and protects your personal data.
privacy.s1: YouFly collects the personal data necessary for processing bookings: first name, last name, date of birth, passport number, email address and phone number. We also collect anonymised usage data to improve the service.
privacy.s2Intro: Your data is used exclusively for:
privacy.s2L3: Improving the user experience on the platform
privacy.s3Intro: We share your personal data with:
privacy.s3L1: Duffel Technologies Ltd (payment and booking processor)
privacy.s3Note: We do not sell your personal data to any third party.
privacy.s4: All data is transmitted via encrypted connections (HTTPS/TLS). Payment data is not stored on our servers. We use best security practices to protect data.
privacy.s5: You have the right to: access your personal data, rectify incorrect data, delete data ("right to be forgotten"), data portability, objection to processing. To exercise these rights, contact us at privacy{'@'}youfly.md.
privacy.s6: YouFly uses essential cookies for the platform to function and stores search preferences in localStorage. We do not use tracking or advertising cookies.
